item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.4 No.4-5, 1956
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI's CHRONICLE No. 4-5 (64-65) Vol IV 1956
...nothing affirmeth and therefore never lyeth... Sir Phillip Sidney (1580)
DUBLIN
UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES
sketch_descriptions: A street scene featuring various figures drawn in an expressive, loose style, showing men and women walking and standing near arched doorways or building entrances.
Figures include a man in a long coat and hat, a woman in a dress and wide-brimmed hat, and smaller figures sketched near windows or doors representing people looking from windows.
The architecture is sketched with tall, rectangular window frames, arched doorways, and some indication of columns or gate-like structures.
pages.page_summary: The page presents a loosely sketched street scene in Dublin accompanied by a typeset title and a quotation from Sir Phillip Sidney. It is from Topolski's Chronicle, volume IV, 1956.

pages.handwritten_text:
Find a triple at V\ [?]
The "Shadflies" by the Liffey
Dissi- Market
sketch_descriptions: Top left sketch shows three figures standing in front of a background with some buildings and two smaller figures in the distance.
Bottom left sketch shows a group of people standing closely together outdoors, some wrapped in cloaks or shawls, with faint urban background details and a small dog near the bottom left.
Top right sketch features a close-up of a cow's head surrounded by several people, some wearing hats, curiously looking towards the animal; the scene is captioned 'Dissi-Market'.

Bottom right sketch illustrates an outdoor street scene with four figures in the foreground near a gated fence, with buildings and additional figures in the background; a sign reading 'DOGGETTS' is visible on a building in the distance.
pages.page_summary: This page presents a series of charcoal or ink sketches depicting market scenes and groups of people, including a notable cow at the Dissi Market and a crowd near the Liffey, with handwritten captions naming the locations and subjects.

sketch_descriptions: Black ink sketch of a street scene with people gathered along the sidewalks and buildings on both sides. The left side shows detailed architectural features and groups of people sitting or standing, while the right side features a red-toned building with windows and doorways.
pages.page_summary: The page features a sketch by Felix Topolski depicting a busy street scene in Dublin, with groups of people interacting in an urban setting framed by detailed buildings.

CENSORSHIP BOA
BAN ON 183 BOOKS

pages.handwritten_text:
Magistrate's Court, Dublin
Irish Republic
TB children & sanitorium
The President Mr Jean Thomas O'Kelly
"Father" Paddy Brown of Galway University
Jack Keats
sketch_descriptions: Group scene in a courtroom with several men including a figure in military uniform and a seated judge on a bench.
Profile portrait of an elderly man with glasses, with text identifying him as 'The President Mr Jean Thomas O'Kelly'.
Various sketches of children's faces, labeled TB children & sanitorium.
Portrait sketch of a man in profile with handwritten label 'Jack Keats'.
Profile portrait bust sketch of a man labeled 'Father Paddy Brown of Galway University'.
Large profile sketch of a man seated next to a page of censored Irish Times newspaper text listing banned books.
pages.page_summary: This page features courtroom sketches, portraits including that of President Jean Thomas O'Kelly and Father Paddy Brown, and a censored page from The Irish Times listing 183 banned books during a censorship event.

pages.typeset_text:
"TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 3s. 6d A COPY
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE £4. 4s. ($12 or equivalent). SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION.
SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME
Handprinted in Great Britain by Puets & Painters' Press 146 Sutton Walk, S.E.1. from blocks made by Austin Miles Ltd and published from Bridge Arch 158, Sutton Walk, London, S.E.1. Copyright reserved
pages.handwritten_text:
GAIETY theatre Dublin "The Silver Tassie" by Sean O'Casey
Dublin - on the way to cattle-market
sketch_descriptions: A group portrait of four men, roughly sketched, with expressive faces and hats, captioned as from a theatre scene in Dublin for the play 'The Silver Tassie' by Sean O'Casey.
A busy cattle market scene in Dublin, featuring various animals including cows and pigs, with several human figures and structures in the background.
pages.page_summary: This page features two primary sketches: a theatrical group portrait from the Gaiety Theatre in Dublin illustrating Sean O'Casey's play 'The Silver Tassie,' and a complex scene depicting a busy cattle market in Dublin. It also contains publication details of Topolski's Chronicle.
